    Request Samples

    From Soil to CupKenya's Finest Coffee Regions

    Discover the diverse terroir that makes each Kenyan coffee region unique, from the volcanic soils of Nyeri to the high altitudes of Kirinyaga.

    Why Origin Matters

    Each coffee region tells a unique story through its terroir, influencing every aspect of the final cup from acidity to body to flavor notes.

    Altitude & Climate

    Higher altitudes create denser beans with more complex flavors and pronounced acidity

    Microclimate

    Unique combinations of temperature, rainfall, and humidity shape distinct flavor profiles

    Soil Composition

    Rich volcanic soils provide essential minerals that contribute to coffee's unique characteristics

    Processing Traditions

    Regional expertise in processing methods passed down through generations

    Kenya Coffee Regions Map

    Explore the geographical distribution of Kenya's premier coffee growing regions

    View Region Details

    Featured Coffee Regions

    Our primary sourcing regions known for exceptional quality and consistency

    Nyeri

    Featured

    Renowned for bright acidity and wine-like characteristics

    Altitude:1,200-2,000m
    Main Harvest:Oct-Dec
    Cooperatives:15

    Cup Profile:

    Bright, wine-like with blackcurrant and citrus notes, exceptional acidity

    Explore Nyeri

    Kirinyaga

    Featured

    High altitude coffees from Mount Kenya's slopes with complex fruit flavors

    Altitude:1,300-1,900m
    Main Harvest:Oct-Jan
    Cooperatives:12

    Cup Profile:

    Complex, fruity with wine-like acidity and vibrant citrus undertones

    Explore Kirinyaga

    Kisii

    Featured

    High altitude region producing full-bodied coffees with rich chocolate notes

    Altitude:1,500-2,000m
    Main Harvest:Apr-Jul
    Cooperatives:8

    Cup Profile:

    Full body with chocolate and caramel hints, sweet lingering finish

    Explore Kisii

    Additional Sourcing Regions

    Other quality coffee regions we work with for specialty lots and seasonal offerings

    Bungoma

    Western Kenya region with emerging specialty coffee characteristics

    Altitude1,400-1,800m
    Cooperatives6
    Learn More

    Murang'a

    Traditional coffee region with fertile soils and consistent quality production

    Altitude1,200-1,700m
    Cooperatives10
    Learn More

    Meru

    Premium coffees from Mount Kenya's eastern slopes, known as the champagne region

    Altitude1,300-1,800m
    Cooperatives9
    Learn More

    Quick Region Comparison

    Compare key characteristics across our featured coffee regions

    RegionAltitudeMain HarvestPrimary VarietalsKey Characteristics
    Nyeri1,200-2,000mOct-DecSL28, SL34, Ruiru 11Renowned for bright acidity and wine-like characteristics
    Kirinyaga1,300-1,900mOct-JanSL28, SL34, BatianHigh altitude coffees from Mount Kenya's slopes with complex fruit flavors
    Kisii1,500-2,000mApr-JulSL28, SL34, Ruiru 11, BatianHigh altitude region producing full-bodied coffees with rich chocolate notes

    Discover the Perfect Origin for Your Roastery

    Each region offers unique characteristics. Let us help you find the perfect Kenyan coffee to complement your roasting style and customer preferences.
